headline: Hungary moves from 28.1 to 29.7 — modest, conservative trajectory bump for the confirmed May 9 inaugural session that ends 16 years of Fidesz/Orbán government and seats Péter Magyar as PM with a Tisza two-thirds supermajority. Methodology v1.2 evidence-specific posture: structural governance transition is confirmed (SYS), EU re-alignment trajectory is signaled (BND), but enacted rule-of-law reforms have not yet occurred — INT, ACC, EQU held. Sub-threshold delta of +1.6; confirmation-only; June 9 reassessment scheduled.
key_evidence: May 9 2026: Hungarian President Sulyok convenes inaugural session of new National Assembly at 10am; Péter Magyar elected and sworn in as PM at 3pm. Constitutional transfer of executive power complete.
Tisza Party won 141 of 199 seats April 12 — largest share of votes and seats won by any Hungarian party in a free election. Two-thirds supermajority enables Fundamental Law amendments.
16-ministry cabinet finalized with standalone ministries for education, health, finance, and social affairs — structural decentralization of prior consolidated authority.
Public 'system change' celebration planned in central Budapest (Kossuth Square, Danube embankment) — signals public participation orientation distinct from prior administration.
EU re-alignment expected; rule-of-law restoration anticipated but not yet enacted as of scan date.

evidence.finding: Inaugural session of new National Assembly convened by President Sulyok for May 9 2026. New parliament takes oaths, elects Speaker, forms parliamentary groups. Magyar elected PM same day.
Magyar to be sworn in as Hungary's PM at 3pm May 9; full public celebration planned. Constitutional transfer ends 16 years of Fidesz government.
Tisza won 141 seats out of 199 — largest vote share and seat share won by any Hungarian party in a free election; two-thirds supermajority threshold for Fundamental Law amendments achieved.
16-ministry cabinet structure finalized; standalone ministries for education, health, finance, and social affairs.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 unchanged. No in-window suffering-detection infrastructure changes; reforms are political-structural, not yet operational.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.0 unchanged. No structural empathy-process evidence yet.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 unchanged. Inauguration alone is not actionable harm response; future cycles will assess whether ministerial reorganization improves response capacity.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 unchanged. Anti-LGBTQ legislation and minority-targeting infrastructure from the prior administration remains in statute as of the scan date. Repeal not yet enacted.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.7 (+0.2). B3 Scope Clarity benefits modestly from EU re-alignment trajectory: Magyar has stated commitment to restoring rule-of-law mechanisms previously eroded. Anchor moves toward 3 in part. Bump deliberately small — re-alignment is signaled, not yet enacted.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.0 unchanged. Historical-harm acknowledgment (AB1, AB5) requires post-transition action that has not yet occurred. The Magyar government has signaled accountability for prior corruption but no formal acknowledgment process is in place as of scan date.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.8 (+0.3). S2 Long-Term Impact and S4 Structural Critique benefit from confirmed constitutional transfer of executive power. Two-thirds supermajority enables Fundamental Law amendments — this is the largest structural governance change available in a parliamentary democracy. Anchor moves toward 3 partially. Bump moderate but conservative — institutional implementation has not yet begun.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 unchanged. I5 Resilience of Care: the new government has not yet been tested under stress; values-alignment trajectory cannot be scored until post-formation actions are observed.

math_check.validator_check: Sub-threshold delta (+1.6); within validator 2.0pt cycle ceiling; Developing band sustained.

first_post_inauguration_judgment: Per methodology v1.2 conservative-scoring rule for political transitions: structural events that are confirmed (SYS) get partial credit; trajectory signals that are not yet enacted (BND) get a smaller bump; dimensions that require enacted reform (INT, ACC, EQU) hold. Hungary's 16-year Fidesz era ending is not a small event, but the methodology requires evidence-specific scoring rather than expected-reform scoring. The +1.6 composite reflects the structural transition itself, not the anticipated downstream effects.
negativeSignals: Anti-LGBTQ legislation and minority-targeting policies from prior administration remain in statute; rule-of-law restoration not yet enacted; institutional reform takes time to manifest in measurable outcomes.
positiveSignals: Confirmed constitutional transfer of executive power on May 9; Tisza two-thirds supermajority is the largest in any free Hungarian election; 16-ministry decentralization; EU re-alignment trajectory signaled; public-participation orientation in inauguration design.
sector_context: Hungary is one of two governance-transition assessments this cycle (alongside South Sudan UNMISS leadership change). Both are noted as institutional-trajectory signals, not scorable improvements until operational evidence appears.
open_actions_resolved: Constitutional transfer credited under SYS; EU re-alignment trajectory under BND
INT, ACC, EQU held pending enacted reform
open_actions_carried: Schedule June 9 follow-up cycle for 30-day post-formation re-queue
Monitor for first ministerial actions on rule-of-law restoration
Monitor for first ministerial actions on minority-rights legislation repeal
Monitor for accountability/anti-corruption institutional process establishment
